zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Russell於1606年6月29日出生在英國英格蘭素可洛瑟費爾德時，她的父親William Russell49歲，母親Mary Hilles37歲。她於1629年8月7日與John Catt在英國素可福朗特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1689年10月18日在英國英格蘭素可享年83歲，埋葬在英國素可諾迪亞姆。

nameMeanings.name: Russell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: of Norman origin, from Old French and Anglo-Norman French r(o)ussel, a diminutive of Old French rous(e) ‘red, reddish’, used either as a nickname for someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion, or as a personal name. Compare Rouse . This Norman name has been established in Ireland since the 12th century. It has been reinforced in Britain and Ireland by Huguenot bearers of the name Roussel, of the same Old French origin.

English: habitational name from any of several places called Rushall (Norfolk, Staffordshire, Wiltshire) or possibly sometimes from Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent). Rushall in Staffordshire derives from Old English rysc ‘rush, rushes’ + halh ‘nook, corner of land’. Rushall in Norfolk derives from an uncertain first element + Old English halh. Rushall in Wiltshire derives from an Old English personal name Rust (genitive Rustes) + halh. Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent) probably derives from Old English rust ‘rust, rust color’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’, but with a change in the final element due to influence from Middle English, Old English hall ‘hall, residence’, perhaps referring to a nearby building.
Americanized form of German Rüssel, from a pet form of any of various personal names formed with the element hrōd ‘fame, renown’.

variants.name: Russler
meanings: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of rüsseln ‘to shallow plow’.

variants.name: Rouse
meanings: English: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French rous(e) ‘red(-haired)’ (from Latin russ(e)us).
Walloon and northern French (Rousé): variant of Rosé (see Rose ).

variants.name: Russel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Russell  .
German: from a pet form of Russ, see Russ 2.
northwest German (Rüssel): probably a variant of Rossel .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Russell was born on 29 June 1606, in Rotherfield, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Russell, was 49 and her mother, Mary Hilles, was 37. She married John Catt on 7 August 1629, in Frant, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 18 October 1689, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 83, and was buried in Northiam, Sussex, England.

Elle a épousé John Catt le 7 août 1629, à Frant, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 18 octobre 1689, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à Northiam, Sussex, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Russell(이)가 1606년 6월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 로더필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Russell(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Hilles(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 1629년 8월 7일 영국 수세스 프랜트에서 John Catt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1689년 10월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 노르스이암에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Russell nasceu em 29 de junho de 1606, em Rotherfield, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Russell, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Mary Hilles, tinha 37 anos. Ela casou-se com John Catt em 7 de agosto de 1629, em Frant, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Russell родилась 29 июня 1606 г. в местности ротирфиелд, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Russell было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Hilles, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Catt 7 августа 1629 г. в местности франт, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 18 октября 1689 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности нортям, суссекс, Англия.
